name: auth-patterns
description: Authentication & authorization patterns: OAuth, session-based, RBAC, Firebase Auth, social login

entries.title: Firebase Authentication Setup
entries.content: Firebase Auth provides email/password, Google, GitHub, Apple sign-in. Client-side: initializeApp, signInWithPopup, onAuthStateChanged. Server-side: verify ID tokens with firebase-admin. Store user profile in Firestore or your DB.

entries.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
entries.content: Define roles (admin, editor, viewer) with permissions. Check permissions in middleware. Store roles in user record. Use bitfield or array for multiple roles. Hierarchical: admin > editor > viewer.

entries.title: Session-Based Auth with Cookies
entries.content: Server creates session on login, stores session ID in httpOnly secure cookie. Session data in memory/Redis/DB. Advantages: revocable, no client-side token storage. Set SameSite=Lax for CSRF protection.

entries.title: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow
entries.content: Standard OAuth flow: 1) Redirect user to provider's auth URL, 2) User authorizes, provider redirects back with code, 3) Exchange code for tokens on server, 4) Use access token for API calls. Use PKCE for public clients.
